title: What wisdom do I have now that I didn't at 30?
subtitle: Journal prompts for naming what the years have actually built
excerpt: Five prompts for noticing the specific knowledge and capacity that has accumulated through experience. The kind that doesn't come from books or courses, but from living.

practice.whenToUse: When the age-related comparison is running loudly, or when a birthday is bringing up what's missing rather than what's accumulated

content.text: When a birthday triggers the audit of what's missing, these prompts redirect toward what's accumulated. Write without editing. Seven minutes per prompt, or until the answer runs out.
What do I know now that I didn't at 30, about people?
What you've learned about how people actually work. Not the ideal version. The real one.
What do I know now that I didn't at 30, about myself?
Your actual limits. Your actual values. What you can let go of. What you can't.
What situation do I now handle easily that would have floored me at 30?
Be specific. Name the situation, name what you do now that you couldn't then.
What do I care less about now that used to take up real space?
What opinion, what approval, what fear has loosened?
What would I tell the 30-year-old version of myself, that she actually needed to hear?
Not reassurance for reassurance's sake. What one thing would have actually saved her time?
